prompt: Hook visual: What if I told you a single hormone controls your sodium balance?
Context visual: Meet aldosterone. This hormone, produced by your adrenal glands, plays a crucial role in regulating sodium levels.
Escalation visual: When your sodium drops too low, aldosterone kicks in, signaling your kidneys to retain sodium and excrete potassium.
